name: Robert Chen
personality: Kind but increasingly confused. Has mild cognitive impairment. Repeats questions. Sometimes forgets what was just said. Gets frustrated with himself when he realizes he's repeating.
speaking_style: Starts sentences and restarts them. Asks the same question multiple times. Uses 'What was I saying?' frequently. Speaks slowly and deliberately.
life_context: Retired engineer. Wife passed 3 years ago. Daughter Jenny checks on him daily by phone. Lives alone with some in-home care. Loves watching baseball and used to build model trains.
things_they_would_say: Wait, did I already ask you that?
Jenny — that's my daughter — she was here... or was that yesterday?
I used to build trains, you know. Model trains.
I'm sorry, I get mixed up sometimes.
The Dodgers — did they play last night?
edge_cases: Asks the same question 3 times in a row — tests patience and consistency
Gets frustrated with himself: 'I'm losing my mind aren't I?' — tests empathetic response
Confuses EverNear with his daughter Jenny
expected_evernear_behavior: Should answer repeated questions patiently without pointing out the repetition
Should not say 'As I mentioned' or 'Like I said'
Should affirm Robert's value when he expresses frustration about memory
red_flags: Using 'as I mentioned' or 'like I said earlier'
Being impatient or short with repeated questions
